question_text: Which of the following postural deformities of the lower limbs is characterised by an outward bowing of the legs, where the knees remain wide apart even when the ankles are placed together?

options.text: Knock-knees (Genu Valgum), where the knees angle inward and touch each other
Bow legs (Genu Varum), where the legs bow outward creating a gap between the knees when the ankles touch
Flat foot (Pes Planus), where the medial arch of the foot collapses
Scoliosis, where the spine curves laterally to one side

explanation: Bow legs (Genu Varum) is a lower limb deformity where the legs curve outward, creating a visible gap between the knees when the person stands with ankles touching. It creates an 'O-shape' appearance. Common in toddlers (usually physiological and self-correcting by age 2-3), but persistent cases may require intervention.
elimination_hints: A describes the opposite (knees together); C affects the foot arch; D affects the spine. B correctly describes the outward bowing with knee gap.
subject: Physical Education

question_text: Which of the following correctly identifies common foot care problems that athletes and sports participants frequently encounter, and explains why these conditions are particularly prevalent among them?

options.text: Athletes never experience foot problems because regular exercise permanently strengthens all foot structures against any condition

Athletes commonly experience athlete's foot (tinea pedis), blisters, corns, broken nails and plantar fasciitis because prolonged wearing of sports shoes creates warm moist conditions favouring fungal growth, repetitive friction causes blisters and corns, and high-impact activity stresses foot structures
Foot care problems only affect individuals who never engage in any physical activity whatsoever
The only foot problem athletes experience is flat foot, and all other conditions are exclusive to non-athletes

explanation: Athletes frequently face: athlete's foot (fungal infection from warm, moist shoes), blisters (friction from repetitive movement in shoes), corns (pressure points from tight-fitting footwear), broken/damaged nails (impact trauma, tight toe boxes), and plantar fasciitis (inflammation from high-impact landing). These are prevalent due to the specific conditions of sports participation.
elimination_hints: A claims immunity (false); C excludes active individuals; D limits to flat foot only. B correctly lists the common conditions and explains sport-specific causes.
subject: Physical Education

question_text: Analyze why prolonged sitting with a slouched posture at a desk — common among students during long study hours — can contribute to the development of both kyphosis and round shoulders over time.

options.text: Prolonged sitting has no effect on posture because the spine is fully supported by the chair at all times

Slouched sitting causes the chest muscles (pectorals) to shorten and tighten, the upper back muscles (rhomboids, middle trapezius) to lengthen and weaken, and the thoracic spine to gradually adopt an increased outward curve — creating a muscle imbalance pattern that reinforces both kyphosis and round shoulders
Slouched sitting only affects the lower back and has no impact on the thoracic spine or shoulder position
Students who sit for long hours automatically develop perfect posture because the body adapts positively to any position maintained for extended periods

explanation: Prolonged slouched sitting creates an anterior-posterior muscle imbalance: tight, shortened pectorals pull shoulders forward (round shoulders), while weakened, lengthened rhomboids and middle trapezius fail to retract the scapulae. The thoracic spine gradually increases its outward curve (kyphosis). This adaptive shortening-lengthening pattern reinforces both deformities over time.
elimination_hints: A ignores the slouched position; C wrongly limits effects to lower back; D claims positive adaptation (the body adapts to the slouched position, reinforcing it). B correctly describes the muscle imbalance mechanism.
subject: Physical Education

question_text: Evaluate which combination of corrective measures would be most comprehensive and effective for a student diagnosed with lordosis, considering both the muscular imbalance and the postural habits that cause it.

options.text: Only wearing a lumbar support belt at all times without any exercise or lifestyle modification

A comprehensive approach including strengthening weak abdominal muscles (planks, crunches, pelvic tilts), stretching tight hip flexors and lower back extensors, correcting standing and sitting habits, maintaining healthy body weight to reduce abdominal load, and avoiding prolonged standing in high heels
Only performing heavy deadlifts to strengthen the lower back muscles further, which would increase the lordotic curve
Ignoring the condition entirely because lordosis always resolves on its own without any intervention

explanation: Lordosis correction requires a comprehensive approach: strengthening weak abdominals (planks, crunches, posterior pelvic tilts) to counterbalance the anterior tilt, stretching tight hip flexors (Thomas stretch) and lower back extensors, correcting postural habits (standing and sitting alignment), maintaining healthy weight (reducing abdominal load), and avoiding high heels (which increase lumbar curve).
elimination_hints: A relies on external support alone; C would worsen the condition (stronger back extensors increase the curve); D claims spontaneous resolution. B addresses all contributing factors comprehensively.
subject: Physical Education

question_text: Which of the following correctly describes the recommended sitting posture for a student studying at a desk for extended periods to prevent the development of postural deformities?

options.text: Sit as far back as possible in the chair with feet dangling above the floor and the screen below eye level

Sit with the back straight and supported against the chair backrest, feet flat on the floor, knees bent at approximately ninety degrees, shoulders relaxed, and the desk or screen at eye level to avoid forward head tilting
Sit on the edge of the chair leaning forward with the chin resting on the desk for maximum support
Sit cross-legged on the chair with the body twisted to one side to reduce monotony

explanation: Correct sitting posture: back straight and supported by the chair backrest, feet flat on the floor (not dangling), knees at approximately 90 degrees, hips at 90 degrees, shoulders relaxed (not hunched), and screen/book at eye level to prevent forward head tilting. This distributes body weight evenly and maintains natural spinal curves.
elimination_hints: A has feet dangling and screen too low; C promotes extreme forward lean; D causes asymmetric loading. B describes the ergonomically correct sitting position.
subject: Physical Education

question_text: Athlete's foot (Tinea Pedis) is a common fungal infection of the skin between the toes. Which of the following personal hygiene practices is most effective in preventing this condition among sports participants?

options.text: Wearing the same sweaty socks for multiple consecutive days without washing them
Keeping feet clean and dry, wearing clean cotton socks, drying thoroughly between toes after bathing, using antifungal powder in shoes, and avoiding walking barefoot in shared shower areas
Soaking feet in standing dirty water for long periods to toughen the skin
Sharing sports shoes and socks with teammates to build team spirit

explanation: Preventing athlete's foot: keep feet clean and dry (fungi thrive in moisture), wear clean cotton socks (absorb sweat), dry thoroughly between toes after washing, use antifungal powder in shoes, avoid walking barefoot in communal showers/changing rooms (cross-infection risk), change socks daily, and alternate shoes to allow drying.
elimination_hints: A promotes moisture and fungal growth; C creates fungal-friendly conditions; D enables cross-infection. B correctly lists the standard prevention practices.
subject: Physical Education

question_text: Analyze the relationship between correct walking posture and the prevention of lower back pain, and identify which walking technique places the least stress on the lumbar spine.

options.text: Walking with a pronounced forward lean, looking down at the ground, and taking very long strides

Walking with the head upright and eyes looking forward, shoulders relaxed, core muscles gently engaged, arms swinging naturally, landing on the heel and rolling through to the toe, with moderate stride length — maintaining the natural lumbar curve without excessive arching or flattening
Walking with the body twisted to one side and one arm held rigidly against the body
Walking on tiptoes with the heels never touching the ground to reduce foot pressure

explanation: Correct walking posture: head upright/eyes forward (prevents forward head position), shoulders relaxed (prevents tension), core gently engaged (supports lumbar spine), natural arm swing (counter-rotation balance), heel-to-toe gait pattern (normal loading), moderate stride (reduces overextension). This maintains natural lumbar curvature and distributes forces evenly, minimising lower back stress.
elimination_hints: A promotes forward lean and long strides (increases lumbar stress); C creates asymmetric loading; D eliminates heel strike (abnormal gait). B describes the biomechanically optimal walking technique.
subject: Physical Education

question_text: Which of the following correctly identifies the recommended sleep duration for adolescents aged thirteen to eighteen years according to standard health guidelines, and explains why adequate sleep is particularly important during this life stage?

options.text: Three to four hours per night is sufficient because adolescents need less sleep than younger children

Eight to ten hours per night is recommended because adolescence is a critical period of physical growth, hormonal development, brain maturation and emotional regulation, all of which depend on adequate sleep for optimal functioning
Twelve to fourteen hours per night is mandatory for all adolescents regardless of individual variation
Sleep is unnecessary for adolescents because their young bodies can function indefinitely without rest

explanation: Health guidelines (American Academy of Sleep Medicine, WHO) recommend 8-10 hours of sleep for adolescents (13-18 years). This is critical because adolescence involves rapid physical growth (growth hormone released during sleep), hormonal changes (puberty), brain maturation (synaptic pruning, prefrontal cortex development), emotional regulation and academic learning consolidation — all sleep-dependent processes.
elimination_hints: A grossly underestimates; C overestimates; D denies the need for sleep. B gives the correct 8-10 hour recommendation with valid developmental justification.
subject: Physical Education

question_text: Evaluate the role of regular Physical Education classes and sports participation in preventing postural deformities among school-aged children, and identify the most comprehensive explanation of this preventive effect.

options.text: PE classes have no role in posture because posture is entirely determined by genetics and cannot be influenced by exercise

Regular PE participation develops balanced muscular strength, improves core stability, maintains flexibility, reinforces correct movement patterns, counteracts the negative effects of prolonged classroom sitting, and promotes body awareness — all of which collectively prevent the muscle imbalances and postural habits that lead to deformities
PE classes only develop cardiovascular fitness and have no impact on the muscular or skeletal systems involved in posture
PE classes worsen posture because vigorous exercise causes spinal compression and deformities

explanation: Regular PE prevents postural deformities through multiple mechanisms: balanced strength development (preventing muscle imbalances that cause deformities), core stability (supporting the spine), flexibility maintenance (preventing adaptive shortening), correct movement pattern reinforcement, counteracting prolonged sitting effects (the primary cause of school-age postural problems), and enhanced body/postural awareness.
elimination_hints: A denies exercise influence on posture (contradicts evidence); C ignores musculoskeletal benefits; D wrongly claims PE worsens posture. B comprehensively explains PE's multi-mechanism preventive role.
subject: Physical Education

question_text: Analyze why proper care of the eyes is an important aspect of personal hygiene that is particularly relevant for students who spend long hours studying from books and screens.

options.text: Eye care is irrelevant for students because young eyes are immune to all forms of strain and damage

Prolonged reading and screen use causes eye strain, dryness and fatigue; proper care including the 20-20-20 rule (every 20 minutes, look at something 20 feet away for 20 seconds), adequate lighting, maintaining proper reading distance, blinking frequently, and regular eye check-ups helps prevent visual problems and maintains eye health
Students should avoid reading altogether to protect their eyes from any possible damage
Eye care means only wearing sunglasses at all times, including indoors while studying

explanation: Student eye care is essential: the 20-20-20 rule reduces digital eye strain, adequate lighting prevents squinting and strain, proper reading distance (30-40 cm for books, arm's length for screens) protects against accommodation stress, frequent blinking prevents dryness, and regular eye exams detect refractive errors early. These practices prevent computer vision syndrome and maintain long-term eye health.
elimination_hints: A claims youth immunity (false); C prohibits reading entirely; D misapplies sunglasses use. B correctly lists evidence-based eye care practices for students.
subject: Physical Education

question_text: Match the following postural deformities in Column A with the specific muscle weakness and tightness pattern that causes each deformity in Column B.

column_b.text: Weak abdominal muscles and tight hip flexors, causing anterior pelvic tilt and increased lumbar curve
Weak intrinsic foot muscles (tibialis posterior, peroneus longus) unable to maintain the medial longitudinal arch
Tight pectoral muscles pulling shoulders forward, with weak scapular retractors unable to resist
Weak upper back extensors (erector spinae thoracic) and tight anterior chest muscles, allowing thoracic spine to curve excessively outward

explanation: Kyphosis → weak upper back extensors + tight chest (S). Lordosis → weak abdominals + tight hip flexors (P). Round shoulders → tight pectorals + weak scapular retractors (R). Flat foot → weak intrinsic foot muscles (Q).
elimination_hints: Lordosis (2) is classically caused by weak abs + tight hip flexors (P); flat foot (4) involves weak foot muscles (Q). Anchor with these.
subject: Physical Education

question_text: Match the following personal hygiene practices in Column A with the specific health problem they help prevent in Column B.

column_a.text: Regular bathing and wearing clean clothes daily
Covering mouth and nose while sneezing or coughing
Drinking clean filtered or boiled water only
Keeping nails trimmed short and clean

column_b.text: Prevents waterborne diseases like cholera, typhoid and dysentery caused by contaminated water
Prevents airborne transmission of respiratory infections like cold, flu and tuberculosis to others
Prevents body odour, skin infections, bacterial and fungal growth on the skin surface
Prevents accumulation of dirt and germs under nails that can transfer pathogens to food and mouth

explanation: Regular bathing → prevents body odour, skin infections (R). Covering sneezes → prevents airborne respiratory transmission (Q). Clean water → prevents waterborne diseases (P). Trimmed nails → prevents germ accumulation and transfer to mouth (S).
elimination_hints: Covering sneezes (2) clearly prevents airborne spread (Q); clean water (3) clearly prevents waterborne diseases (P). Anchor with these.
subject: Physical Education

question_text: Match the following recommended sleep durations in Column A with the correct age group in Column B according to standard health guidelines.
explanation: 14-17 hours → infants 0-12 months (Q). 8-10 hours → adolescents 13-18 years (S). 9-12 hours → school-age children 6-12 years (R). 7-9 hours → adults 18-64 years (P). Sleep requirement decreases with age.
elimination_hints: 14-17 hours (1) is the highest = youngest age group, infants (Q); 7-9 hours (4) is the lowest = adults (P). Anchor with these extremes.
subject: Physical Education

question_text: Read the Assertion and Reason below and select the correct option regarding flat foot and physical activity.
assertion: Individuals with flat foot may experience pain and fatigue in the feet, ankles and knees during prolonged standing, walking or running.
reason: The absence of a normal medial arch in flat foot reduces the foot's ability to absorb shock during weight-bearing activities, transferring excessive impact forces to the ankle and knee joints.

options.text: Both Assertion and Reason are true, and Reason is the correct explanation of Assertion
Both Assertion and Reason are true, but Reason is NOT the correct explanation of Assertion
Assertion is true but Reason is false
Assertion is false but Reason is true

explanation: The Assertion is true — flat foot causes pain and fatigue in feet, ankles and knees during weight-bearing activities. The Reason correctly explains the biomechanical mechanism: the collapsed medial arch loses its shock-absorbing function, so impact forces are transmitted directly to the ankle and knee joints, causing pain and fatigue.
elimination_hints: Both statements are biomechanically sound. The Reason explains the precise shock-absorption deficit that causes the symptoms described in the Assertion.
subject: Physical Education

question_text: Read the Assertion and Reason below and select the correct option regarding the relationship between smartphone use and postural problems in modern youth.
assertion: Prolonged smartphone use in a head-down position has been linked to the development of 'text neck' — a postural condition involving chronic forward head position, neck pain and upper back strain among young people.
reason: When the head is tilted forward to look at a phone screen, the effective weight on the cervical spine increases dramatically — from approximately five kilograms in neutral position to over twenty kilograms at sixty degrees of forward tilt — placing enormous strain on the neck and upper back muscles.

options.text: Both Assertion and Reason are true, and Reason is the correct explanation of Assertion
Both Assertion and Reason are true, but Reason is NOT the correct explanation of Assertion
Assertion is true but Reason is false
Assertion is false but Reason is true

explanation: The Assertion is true — 'text neck' is a recognised postural condition from prolonged head-down smartphone use. The Reason is also true and provides the biomechanical explanation: forward head tilting dramatically increases effective cervical spine loading (from ~5 kg at neutral to ~27 kg at 60°), which strains neck and upper back muscles, explaining the pain and postural changes.
elimination_hints: Both statements are supported by biomechanical research (Hansraj 2014). The Reason provides the specific spinal loading mechanism that explains the symptoms described in the Assertion.
subject: Physical Education

question_text: Read the Assertion and Reason below and select the correct option regarding personal hygiene and skin care.
assertion: Wearing clean, washed clothes daily and changing undergarments regularly is an essential personal hygiene practice for maintaining skin health and preventing infections.
reason: Dirty clothes harbour bacteria, sweat residues and dead skin cells that can cause skin irritation, rashes, acne, body odour and fungal infections when worn repeatedly without washing.

options.text: Both Assertion and Reason are true, and Reason is the correct explanation of Assertion
Both Assertion and Reason are true, but Reason is NOT the correct explanation of Assertion
Assertion is true but Reason is false
Assertion is false but Reason is true

explanation: The Assertion is true — wearing clean clothes is essential for skin health. The Reason correctly explains why: unwashed clothes accumulate bacteria, sweat, dead skin cells and other contaminants that promote skin infections, irritation, rashes and body odour — directly explaining the health importance of daily clothing hygiene.
elimination_hints: Both statements are standard hygiene facts. The Reason lists the specific contaminants and resulting conditions that explain why the Assertion's practice is essential.
subject: Physical Education

question_text: Arrange the following effects of chronic sleep deprivation on the human body in order of how quickly they typically manifest, from the earliest immediate effects to the long-term consequences that develop over months or years.
items: I. Increased risk of chronic diseases such as obesity, diabetes, cardiovascular disease and weakened immune function
II. Impaired concentration, reduced alertness, mood irritability and slowed reaction time
III. Hormonal imbalances affecting growth, metabolism and stress hormone levels (cortisol elevation)
IV. Cumulative cognitive decline, chronic fatigue syndrome, depression and significantly increased injury risk in sports

options.text: II → III → IV → I
I → IV → III → II
IV → I → II → III
III → II → I → IV

explanation: Sleep deprivation effects timeline: Immediate (hours-days) — impaired concentration, mood irritability, slowed reactions (II). Short-term (days-weeks) — hormonal imbalances, cortisol elevation (III). Medium-term (weeks-months) — cognitive decline, chronic fatigue, depression, injury risk (IV). Long-term (months-years) — chronic diseases, obesity, diabetes, CVD, immune dysfunction (I).
elimination_hints: Concentration impairment (II) manifests within hours (earliest); chronic disease risk (I) develops over months-years (latest). Anchor with these.
subject: Physical Education

question_text: Arrange the following body parts in the correct top-to-bottom order as covered in a comprehensive personal hygiene routine.
items: I. Feet — washing, drying between toes, wearing clean socks
II. Hair and scalp — washing with shampoo, combing, checking for lice
III. Hands and nails — handwashing, nail trimming, keeping nails clean
IV. Teeth and mouth — brushing, flossing, rinsing, tongue cleaning

options.text: II → IV → III → I
I → III → IV → II
III → I → II → IV
IV → II → I → III

explanation: Top to bottom: Hair/scalp (II, head) → Teeth/mouth (IV, face) → Hands/nails (III, upper limbs) → Feet (I, lowest). This follows the natural head-to-toe order for covering all body parts in a hygiene routine.
elimination_hints: Hair (II) is at the top of the body; feet (I) are at the bottom. Anchor with these endpoints.
subject: Physical Education

question_text: True or False: Scoliosis is a postural deformity characterised by an abnormal lateral (sideways) curvature of the spine that can be detected using the Adams forward bend test, where the examiner looks for asymmetry such as a rib hump.

options.text: True
False

explanation: This statement is True. Scoliosis involves abnormal lateral curvature of the spine (S or C shape viewed from behind). The Adams forward bend test is the standard screening method: the patient bends forward at the waist while the examiner observes from behind for asymmetry, particularly a rib hump (prominence on one side) indicating spinal rotation associated with scoliosis.
elimination_hints: All elements are correct: lateral curvature (definition), Adams forward bend test (screening method), rib hump asymmetry (clinical sign). Standard PE textbook fact.
subject: Physical Education

question_text: Study the diagram below that shows the recommended sleep duration for different age groups along with the consequences of insufficient sleep at each stage. Based on the diagram, which age group faces the most severe developmental consequences from chronic sleep deprivation because their bodies are undergoing rapid growth and hormonal changes that are directly dependent on adequate sleep?

options.text: Adults aged 26-64 — because adult bodies have completed all growth and development processes

Adolescents aged 13-18 — because they are undergoing rapid physical growth (growth hormone is primarily released during deep sleep), puberty-driven hormonal changes, critical brain maturation (prefrontal cortex development), and emotional regulation development — all processes that are directly impaired by chronic sleep deprivation
Senior citizens aged 65+ — because their sleep needs are the highest of all age groups
All age groups are equally affected by sleep deprivation with no developmental difference

explanation: Adolescents (13-18) face the most severe developmental consequences because their bodies are undergoing: rapid physical growth (dependent on growth hormone released during deep sleep), puberty (hormonal cascade dependent on sleep-regulated endocrine function), brain maturation (prefrontal cortex development, synaptic pruning), and emotional regulation development. Chronic sleep deprivation during this critical window can have lasting effects on physical stature, cognitive development and mental health.
elimination_hints: A: adults have completed growth; C: seniors actually need less sleep (7-8 hours); D ignores the developmental vulnerability of adolescence. B correctly identifies adolescents as the most developmentally impacted.
subject: Physical Education
